title: the surge of populism and the polarization of the society
content: The concept of populismTo kick off this discussion, let’s first explore the concept of populism. Originally associated with the ideas of the US People’s Party founded in 1892, populism has since evolved to encompass mass manipulation. While scholars may debate its precise definition—whether as a phenomenon, ideology, or something else—one defining feature remains: it splits society into “pure” people and “corrupt” elites, applicable to both right- and left-wing populism. Historically, the Jacobins of the French Revolution, led by Robespierre, were among the first populists. After the overthrow of Ancien Régime, the Assembly seized power from that and the Clubs, like Jacobins, emerged. For several reasons, the Assembly failed to meet people‘s expectation about civil rights and inflation rate. Under these circumstances, Jacobins tapped into the citizens’ discontent with the Constitutional Monarchy, exploiting public trust to rally the sans-culottes for violent uprisings. This ultimately led to their overthrow of the monarchy but also spiralled into the Reign of Terror, where ten thousands lost their lives. Even in the modern society, the populism politicians are still renowned to trickily connect the discontents of average citizens and the dominance of elites in commercial, political, and advertising spheres. Take Donald Trump, the ex-president of the States, as a example. Indeed, he has special personal charm to attract supports from citizens among the rural area, this is why he can compete the presidency for three times. However, he utilities the support from citizens to not only strengthen his personal cult but also exacerbate social divisions. According to the Washington Post’s statistics in 4th Oct 2016,Donald Trump knows better than anyone else about 19 things, according to Donald Trump, a mortal guy that is not omniscience. Consequently, After losing the 2020 election, many of his most loyal supporters denied the election’s legitimacy, believing unfounded conspiracy theories that claimed Joe Biden and the Democrats had cheated. This anger over what they perceived as an “unfair” election result culminated in an attack on the Capitol, a symbol of American authority, as they demanded a re-election. By observing both examples in French Revolution and 2020 Capitol Attack, I noticed that it is important to realize the power of populism and try to make it benefit to our society, which leads me to do more research about it. The formation of populism

title: Hello World
content: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub. Quick StartCreate a new post1$ hexo new "My New Post" More info: Writing Run server1$ hexo server More info: Server Generate static files1$ hexo generate More info: Generating Deploy to remote sites1$ hexo deploy More info: Deployment

title: about
content: Here is Oliver Lin’s blog. I am a Grade 12 student who interests in sociology. Here I will post my personal opinions and insights about some certain topic in sociology and the little moments of everyday life. Thanks for reading. When I am free, I will listen some Rap & Jazz music.
